title: Maris original role in the movie
display_title: Maris original role in the movie
wikitext:
[]: Mari's original role in the movie             Quote Post by KaaVI » Sun Nov 28, 2010 4:22 am Ok i came across this in the complete works book and it sorta got me thinking. I you pay attention to the number on the back of her original plusguit design you can see clearly that it is a 4. This is what sparked my thinking on weather or not Mari was supposed to be European at all, or if she was supposed to pilot Unit 04 and be an insignificant character being sent to a dirac sea after the test activation. Either way it seems odd to me that they even considered her a pilot for unit 04. Other things pop up at this time, like if they were going to include some american NERV screen time or if unit 04 was going to be successful in the fact that the s2 engines were successfully made this time round, taking the theory that the movies are sequels.

[]: Re: Mari's original role in the movie             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Nov 28, 2010 5:26 am KaaVI wrote: Either way it seems odd to me that they even considered her a pilot for unit 04.

[]: Re: Mari's original role in the movie             Quote Post by KaaVI » Sun Nov 28, 2010 5:48 am SaltyJoe wrote: I think Sadamoto simply forgot what number goes on the back of the plugsuit when he drew that sketch up.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Nov 28, 2010 11:38 am I'm more interested in the fact that her plugsuit at one point had the same color scheme as Unit 01. TIME-TRAVELING YUI, ANYONE?!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 28, 2010 2:29 pm Read the interview translations from the Complete Records Collection and you'll learn all about the wonderful world of the Mari that might have been.

[]:             Quote Post by DoubleZeva » Sun Nov 28, 2010 2:38 pm It could also have meant the order of the Evas changed. At that point they might have decided that the Nevada Incident happened to another Eva, or not at all. I mean the MP evas appear to have been cut in favor of more Numbered Evas (5, 8, ect), It wouldn't have been a big stretch to say that the ordering of the numbered series could have been changed to, and that Unit05 was going to be unit04 at one point in the design process. Not saying I disagree, just saying there is more than one way to interpret this.

[]: It's a Christmas miracle!             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sun Nov 28, 2010 4:10 pm NAveryW wrote: Read the interview translations from the Complete Records Collection and you'll learn all about the wonderful world of the Mari that might have been.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 28, 2010 4:12 pm Those plugsuit designs look too much like....Kaworu's...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Nov 28, 2010 4:16 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Those plugsuit designs look too much like....Kaworu's...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Nov 28, 2010 5:34 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Those plugsuit designs look too much like....Kaworu's...

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Nov 28, 2010 6:52 pm NAveryW wrote: Read the interview translations from the Complete Records Collection and you'll learn all about the wonderful world of the Mari that might have been.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 29, 2010 11:30 am Aw, do I have to? Just go to the thread titled "Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection".

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 29, 2010 3:05 pm NAveryW wrote: Aw, do I have to? Just go to the thread titled "Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection".

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Nov 29, 2010 3:09 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:05 pm Kendrix wrote: A Mari with less willpower would be no Mari at all.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:53 pm NAveryW wrote: Aw, do I have to? Just go to the thread titled "Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection".

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:54 pm Azathoth wrote: Mari is no Mari at all. We don't have even the slightest idea of who she is or how she behaves apart from being literally insane.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Nov 29, 2010 4:57 pm Kendrix wrote: Isn't that the main question that Evangelion wants to raise? What if Shinji tried? What if they all tried, just a little?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 29, 2010 5:10 pm esselfortium wrote: They did. Did we watch the same NGE?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Nov 29, 2010 5:37 pm Kendrix wrote: Shikinami made me see the potential for Kindness in Soryu

Article: Maris original role in the movie
[]: Mari's original role in the movie             Quote Post by KaaVI » Sun Nov 28, 2010 4:22 am Ok i came across this in the complete works book and it sorta got me thinking. I you pay attention to the number on the back of her original plusguit design you can see clearly that it is a 4. This is what sparked my thinking on weather or not Mari was supposed to be European at all, or if she was supposed to pilot Unit 04 and be an insignificant character being sent to a dirac sea after the test activation. Either way it seems odd to me that they even considered her a pilot for unit 04. Other things pop up at this time, like if they were going to include some american NERV screen time or if unit 04 was going to be successful in the fact that the s2 engines were successfully made this time round, taking the theory that the movies are sequels.

Article: Maris original role in the movie
 It could also have meant the order of the Evas changed. At that point they might have decided that the Nevada Incident happened to another Eva, or not at all. I mean the MP evas appear to have been cut in favor of more Numbered Evas (5, 8, ect), It wouldn't have been a big stretch to say that the ordering of the numbered series could have been changed to, and that Unit05 was going to be unit04 at one point in the design process. Not saying I disagree, just saying there is more than one way to interpret this.
